title: ASOF Joins with OR conditions
description:
### What happens?

I get different results from an AsOf Join with condition `(tt1.j = tt2.j OR tt1.j = tt2.j) AND tt1.i >= tt2.i` compared to the condition `tt1.j = tt2.j AND tt1.i >= tt2.i`. I would expected the same as the left and right sides of the OR are the same.

Is an OR allowed in AsOf Joins?

### To Reproduce

I tried it out using your unit test framework. The tests pass like this. I think the last query output is wrong:

```sql
statement ok
CREATE TABLE tt1 (i INTEGER, j VARCHAR);

statement ok
 INSERT INTO tt1 VALUES 
     (2, 'A'),
     (4, 'B'),
     (5, 'A');

statement ok
CREATE TABLE tt2 (i INTEGER, j VARCHAR, k VARCHAR);

statement ok
INSERT INTO tt2 VALUES 
	(1, 'A', 'I'),
	(3, 'B', 'II');

# This worked as expected.
query II
SELECT tt1.i, tt2.k
   FROM tt1
   ASOF JOIN tt2 ON
  tt1.j = tt2.j AND tt1.i >= tt2.i
  ORDER BY tt1.i;
----
2	I
4	II
5	I

# Here, I expected the same output as before but I get less.
query II
SELECT tt1.i, tt2.k
   FROM tt1
   ASOF JOIN tt2 ON
  (tt1.j = tt2.j OR tt1.j = tt2.j) AND tt1.i >= tt2.i
  ORDER BY tt1.i;
----
2	I
4	II
```

### OS:

macOS 15.5 aarch64

### DuckDB Version:

641e95d140ca7728085445a919c3e8d436aaf0c1

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE tt3 (i INT, j VARCHAR(255), k FLOAT);
CREATE TABLE tt4 (i INT, j VARCHAR(255), k DATE);
SELECT tt3.i, tt4.k FROM tt3 JOIN tt4 ON (tt3.j = tt4.j OR tt3.j IS NULL) AND tt3.i >= tt4.i ORDER BY tt3.i;
key_bug_features: ASOF Join with OR conditions on identical columns
